title: Fix admin login visibility, post-login redirects, and admin-only navbar links

requirements.summary: Ensure admin login option is clearly visible and accessible on the LoginPage without requiring special steps
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /login shows both user and admin login options without any extra interaction
The admin login toggle/tab/button is rendered in the DOM and not hidden by CSS or conditional logic on initial load
Switching between user and admin login modes works correctly

file_operations.description: Update the LoginPage component to ensure both 'User Login' and 'Admin Login' modes are immediately visible on page load. Remove any conditional rendering that hides the admin login toggle on initial render. Ensure the tab/button UI for switching between user and admin modes is always rendered in the DOM and styled to be clearly visible. Verify that the isAdminMode state toggle functionality remains intact and that both modes can be accessed by clicking the respective tab or button.

requirements.summary: Implement correct post-login redirects for admin and regular users after successful authentication
acceptanceCriteria: Admin login flow ends with a redirect to /admin/dashboard
New user login redirects to /register if no profile exists
No redirect loops or stalls occur after login

file_operations.description: Add redirect logic after successful OTP verification in the handleOtpVerify function. Use the useNavigate hook from TanStack Router. After OTP verification succeeds and identity is confirmed: (1) For admin login mode (isAdminMode === true), navigate to '/admin/dashboard'. (2) For regular user login mode, fetch the user profile using the existing useGetCallerUserProfile hook. If the profile is null, navigate to '/register'. If the profile exists, navigate to '/dashboard'. Ensure no redirect occurs until both authentication and profile check (for regular users) are complete. Handle loading states appropriately to prevent premature redirects.

Review and ensure the useGetCallerUserProfile hook properly handles the loading and fetching states to prevent race conditions during login redirects. Confirm that the hook returns accurate isLoading and isFetched states that can be used in LoginPage to determine when profile data is ready for redirect decisions. No changes may be needed if the hook already handles this correctly, but verify the implementation aligns with the redirect logic requirements.

requirements.summary: Show admin-only navigation links in the Navbar only when the authenticated user is confirmed to be an admin
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated (guest) users see no admin links in the Navbar
Regular authenticated (non-admin) users see no admin links in the Navbar
Authenticated admin users see admin-specific navigation links in the Navbar
Admin link visibility updates correctly upon login and logout

file_operations.description: Update the Navbar component to conditionally render admin-only links (e.g., Admin Dashboard, Document Review, Death Verification, Legal Verification, Controlled Access Release) based on the user's admin status. Use the existing useIsCallerAdmin hook from useQueries.ts to determine if the current user is an admin. Only render admin navigation items when isAuthenticated is true AND isAdmin is true. Ensure the admin status check respects the loading state of useIsCallerAdmin to prevent flickering or incorrect initial rendering. Verify that admin links disappear immediately upon logout when the queryClient is cleared.
